sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient is a woman who has been experiencing headaches for years, starting in her teenage years, initially related to her menstrual cycle, but more recently worsening in frequency and severity.
She reports a pulsing sensation on her right side, behind her eye, and notes that her headaches are triggered by exercise and stress.
She also reports tinnitus and blurred vision, particularly on the side of her vision, but denies numbness or tingling in her hands or feet.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient reports tinnitus and blurred vision.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's heart exam is normal, with a normal sinus rhythm, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops.
The funduscopic exam shows papilledema.
The neurological exam is normal, with intact cranial nerves one through twelve and normal cranial reflexes.
The musculoskeletal exam is normal, with a normal gait.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are concerning for idiopathic intracranial hypertension, a condition characterized by increased pressure in the brain without a known cause.
Problem list: Idiopathic intracranial hypertension; suspected; confirmed.
Plan:
I want to order an MRI to confirm the diagnosis and rule out other contributing conditions.
I will also perform a spinal tap to evaluate the pressure in the brain and potentially alleviate symptoms.
The patient will be referred to a neuro-ophthalmologist for a complete eye exam.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with the office to discuss the results of the MRI and schedule the spinal tap and neuro-ophthalmology appointment.
The office will obtain preapproval from the insurance company for the MRI.
